title: `check_compound_complex` leaks 4 HDF5 type handles per call
author: devdanzin
body:
`H5Tget_member_type()` results are used inline in a boolean expression and discarded without `H5Tclose`. Each call leaks 4 type handles.
- `_conv.templ.pyx:915-927`

comments.author: neutrinoceros
comments.body:
The fix isn't clear to me: 
[`H5Tget_member_type`'s documentation](https://support.hdfgroup.org/documentation/hdf5/latest/group___c_o_m_p_o_u_n_d.html#gaf5de0eabe28246f040342e275b9a63eb) clearly mentions that
> The caller should invoke [H5Tclose()](https://support.hdfgroup.org/documentation/hdf5/latest/group___h5_t.html#gafcba4db244f6a4d71e99c6e72b8678f0) to release resources associated with the type.

and following this thread:
> Further access through this datatype identifier is illegal. 

The problem is that `check_compound_complex` never owns the datatype it's calling `H5Tget_member_type` on, so I'm not sure if it should call `H5Tclose` directly or differ to *its* callers... and then I find that
`complex2compound` and `compound2complex` contain the only call sites, but they *also* don't own the datatype they're passing. This may be just be confusing to *me* as I'm discovering most of the API I'm trying to exploring here, but I think I reached the point where I need a hand. @takluyver would be the obvious person to ask as he wrote this code a couple months back.

comments.author: takluyver
comments.body: My reading of that is that `check_compound_complex` itself should close the member type IDs that it gets, but not the main type ID that it's called with. I'll have a go at a fix.
